user_message: How should I coach Ahmed Hassan on Parnell Aerospace? Multiple stakeholders are involved -- it's complex.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-15 15:29 EST
Subject: Re: Re: competitive positioning Timeline

Just checking in on the competitive positioning proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
Date: 2024-01-02 14:53 EST
Subject: competitive positioning - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with competitive positioning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: competitive positioning Discussion - Parnell Aerospace
Date: 2024-02-19
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), David (Parnell Aerospace)

[00:02:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on competitive positioning from our last session.

[00:05:00] David (Parnell Aerospace): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:10:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] David (Parnell Aerospace): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:20:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:23:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
